ranked_movies.title: Pride and Prejudice
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed across multiple threads, with both the 1995 and 2005 versions being passionately debated. It is frequently cited as a top favorite and a benchmark for the romance genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Notebook
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a polarizing film; many users cite it as the 'worst' or 'most overrated' romance movie, generating significant discussion about its merits and flaws.

ranked_movies.title: La La Land
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a personal favorite by multiple users for its bittersweet, musical take on romance. It is recommended for its unique style and emotional impact.

ranked_movies.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended as a top-tier romantic comedy with a stellar cast and a fun, multi-dimensional plot. It is praised for its humor and heart.

ranked_movies.title: Stardust
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in multiple threads. Users love its blend of fantasy and romance, and it is often cited as a favorite for its charming and transformative love story.

ranked_movies.title: While You Were Sleeping
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a charming and adorable comfort film. Sandra Bullock's performance and the feel-good family dynamic are highlighted as key strengths.

ranked_movies.title: Notting Hill
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a classic 'OG' romance, especially for the dynamic of a famous woman with an ordinary man. It is praised for its fresh and original feel.

ranked_movies.title: Long Shot
ranked_movies.reason: Surprised users with its fresh and hilarious take on romance. The chemistry between Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen is frequently praised.

ranked_movies.title: The Proposal
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for the 'powerful woman/ordinary man' dynamic. It is described as a cute and enjoyable film with strong leads.

ranked_movies.title: Love Actually
ranked_movies.reason: A contentious but frequently discussed film. Some users list it as their favorite, while others criticize it for being a trope-filled cliché-fest, generating much debate.

ranked_movies.title: Before Sunrise
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its realistic and heavy portrayal of romance, unlike most other romantic movies. The entire 'Before' series is held in high regard.

ranked_movies.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
ranked_movies.reason: A classic teen romance that is mentioned both as a mid-tier film by some and a great example of a 'toxic lead who changes' by others.

ranked_movies.title: About Time
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its bittersweet and heartfelt story. It is noted for its unique time-travel premise within a romantic framework.

ranked_movies.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a contender for a favorite romance due to its unique, non-traditional, and thought-provoking take on love and memory.

ranked_movies.title: She's Out of My League
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a fitting example of a movie where a powerful female lead is with an ordinary guy. It is cited as an all-time favorite by some users.

ranked_movies.title: 27 Dresses
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a solid, enjoyable romantic comedy that users rewatch and rate highly. It fits a common trope discussed in the threads.

ranked_movies.title: The Shape of Water
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a favorite by a user who relates to the protagonist. Its odd, fantastical nature makes it a unique entry in the romance genre.

ranked_movies.title: A Silent Voice
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically mentioned in an anime context as one of the few romance movies with a definitively happy ending, making it a notable recommendation.

ranked_movies.title: Just Like Heaven
ranked_movies.reason: Identified as the answer to a 'tipofmytongue' query, confirming its place in the cultural conversation around romantic movies with a supernatural twist.

ranked_movies.title: The Kiss Quotient
ranked_movies.reason: The novel is highly requested for a film adaptation, with specific fan casting (Manny Jacinto). Its popularity in the book community signals high potential audience interest.
